title: Gastrochilus rostellatus
taxonomy: Gastrochilus rostellatus is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This family consists of more than 30,000 known species, making it the largest family of flowering plants. Gastrochilus rostellatus is further classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Gastrochilus is a group of orchids that are primarily found in Asia and are known for their unique flowers. The rostellum is a distinctive feature of this species and gives it its name.

It is said to possess antidiabetic properties, helping to regulate blood sugar levels and manage symptoms of diabetes. parts: Gastrochilus rostellatus, a species of orchid native to India and Sri Lanka, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots are reported to possess bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal parts of the plant are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other secondary metabolites, which are believed to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. intro: Gastrochilus rostellatus, also known as the helmet orchid, is a species of flowering orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Gastrochilus rostellatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gastrochilus rostellatus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make various herbal formulations, including capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
